title: A Prayer
content:
O Lord, the hard-won miles
    Have worn my stumbling feet:
    Oh, soothe me with thy smiles,
    And make my life complete.

    The thorns were thick and keen
    Where'er I trembling trod;
    The way was long between
    My wounded feet and God.

    Where healing waters flow
    Do thou my footsteps lead.
    My heart is aching so;
    Thy gracious balm I need.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Paul Laurence Dunbar, titled "A Prayer", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
